enemy.name: The Curriculum

intent.telegraph: Final Exam: Shard storm (4 hits × 4 dmg)

enemy.special: Phase 2: _quickPlayDamageMultiplierOverride = 0.0 (Quick Play deals ZERO damage). Only Charged attacks work. Also +2 enrageBonusDamage from phase transition.

hand.name: Strike
hand.description: Deal damage. [Quick Play deals 0 damage vs Curriculum in phase 2]

hand.name: Strike
hand.description: Deal damage. [Quick Play deals 0 damage vs Curriculum in phase 2]

hand.name: Heavy Strike
hand.description: High damage. [Quick Play deals 0 damage vs Curriculum in phase 2]

relics.name: Crit Lens
relics.description: Charged correct answers have 25% chance to DOUBLE final damage (after all multipliers).

notes: The Curriculum in phase 2: Quick Play deals ZERO damage. Player must Charge to deal any damage. Has 3 AP but Charge costs +1 AP (surcharge). So with 3 AP: can only play 1 Charge card (1 AP base + 1 surcharge = 2 AP, leaving 1 AP for block) OR 1 heavy Charge (2 AP base + 1 surcharge = 3 AP = all AP, no block). Incoming 4×4=16 dmg minus 6 block = 10 through. Player at 22 HP. Every Quick Play card is essentially a dead card here. Critical mechanic-exposure test: does the player understand why Quick Plays are useless?
